response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack software engineer (web, mobile, backend) with strong testing and craftsmanship credentials, but the role is a Principal-level hardware performance architect focused on SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator optimization, and deep-learning workload analysis. There is essentially no overlap: no SoC/IP modeling, no hardware-software co-design, no GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture work, and no deep neural network training/inference optimization experience. Brief C/C++ and embedded exposure does not approach the specialized hardware-architecture depth this principal IC role demands.
strengths: Deep, broad software engineering experience including some C/C++ and embedded work
Strong testing, TDD, and software-craftsmanship discipline
Demonstrated low-latency, high-throughput systems work at scale
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware-software co-design experience
No AI accelerator, GPU, or deep-learning workload optimization background
Career centered on web/full-stack, not chip architecture or performance modeling

key_factors.factor: Lack of SoC/IP modeling & hardware architecture
No AI accelerator / deep-learning optimization experience
